title: Debugging guidance was corrected to trace from the named disagreement variable and prune redundant predicates
expected_state: The debugging guidance should have traced root cause directly from the `interpolated frames` message back to what caused that specific message, and the prompt should have been pruned so lower-level predicates derivable from higher-level ones did not accumulate redundantly.
actual_state: The guidance still allowed looser inference instead of tracing specifically from the named `interpolated frames` disagreement variable, and it still carried enough lower-level predicates that the human had to correct the method to keep instruction following from getting heavier and less sharp.
human_costs: the human had to restate the desired debugging method at a more exact variable-provenance level
the human had to defend against prompt bloat from redundant lower-level constraints

human_intervention.summary: The human tightened the debugging contract by requiring explicit tracing from the `interpolated frames` message and by asking for derivable lower-level predicates to be deleted so the prompt stayed sharper.

feedback_quotes: trace root cause from the interpolated frames message to what's causing _that_ specifically
delete any lower-level predicates that seem derivable from higher-level predicates
I'm concerned that instruction following is going to pessimize by adding to many constraints.

entries.target_state: The debugging method should trace from the named `interpolated frames` message back to the code path that specifically causes that message.
The debugging contract should follow the concrete disagreement variable directly instead of skipping to a looser inference from code read alone.
The prompt should delete lower-level predicates that are already derivable from higher-level rules instead of stacking them redundantly.
Instruction guidance should stay sharp rather than getting heavier and less reliable from too many overlapping constraints.
